items.title: Top AI trends shaping analytics in 2026 (preview)
items.author: Redpanda Data
items.summary:
AI agents are moving faster than most governance models can keep up with. Gartner called in their "Predicts 2026 report" — and so did we.  

In this tech talk, Peter Corless unpacks the Gartner report and shares:

- The AI trends reshaping analytics teams right now
- The skills analysts will need in an agentic world
- How the Redpanda Agentic Data Plane helps you run and govern agentic systems without sacrificing control

Watch the full talk on demand: https://www.redpanda.com/events/predicts2026-tech-talk?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=organic_social&utm_campaign=2026_mar_predict26ai_social

items.title: How to Tune Queries Fast
items.author: Brent Ozar Unlimited
items.summary: Brent Ozar will show you how to use the latest features of sp_BlitzCache to rapidly improve performance on an existing server. He'll show you how to find your server's top bottleneck, find the queries causing that bottleneck, and then demonstrate how to get advice from AI on how to tune them in seconds.

items.title: SQL Server Performance Office Hours Episode 66
items.author: Erik Darling (Erik Darling Data)
items.summary:
In this video, I dive into a variety of SQL Server topics during our office hours session. We tackled questions ranging from adding 'created' and 'last updated' columns to transactional tables using triggers or temporal tables, to troubleshooting performance issues that only occur once a week for 10 minutes. I also discussed why indexing foreign keys can significantly improve delete operations and when dynamic SQL might be worse than static SQL in terms of performance. If you're looking to dive deeper into these topics or need help with your own SQL Server challenges, check out the links in the video description where you can hire me for consulting or purchase my training. Whether youâ€™re a seasoned professional or just starting out, thereâ€™s always something new to learn!

CHAPTERS

00:00:00 - Introduction to Performance Troubleshooting
00:02:34 - Why Does a Big Machine Suddenly Start Choosing Serial Plans?
00:06:27 - Troubleshooting Weekly Performance Issues
00:10:27 - When Does Dynamic SQL Become Worse Than Static SQL?
00:11:28 - Indexing Foreign Keys and Delete Performance

items.title: Apache Kafka 4.3 | 25 KIPs, Kafka Streams, Share Group Controls, Broker Isolation, and more
items.author: Confluent Developer
items.summary:
Blog: https://kafka.apache.org/blog/2026/05/22/apache-kafka-4.3.0-release-announcement/ | Sandon Jacobs (Senior Developer Advocate, Confluent) highlights the key features in Apache Kafka 4.3, a release powered by 25 new KIPs. Apache Kafka 4.3 includes first-class support for preserving record headers in Kafka Streams (KIPs 1271, 1285), new production tuning controls for share groups (KIP-1240), and a new broker and log directory cordoning mechanism to simplify maintenance workflows (KIP-1066). The release also brings improvements to Kafka Streams state management, local state cleanup, and global table exception handling (KIPs 1035, 1259, 1270), plus broader enhancements to metrics, storage, operations, and the path toward Apache Kafka 5.0. For a comprehensive overview, view the release notes: https://downloads.apache.org/kafka/4.3.0/RELEASE_NOTES.html

LEARN MORE
► Queues for Kafka Lightboard: https://youtu.be/Wb0xyqgaIqw
► Apache Release notes: https://kafka.apache.org/downloads
► Confluent Developer: https://developer.confluent.io

CONNECT
Subscribe, if you dare: https://www.youtube.com/@ConfluentDeveloper?sub_confirmation=1
Community Slack: https://confluentcommunity.slack.com
X: https://x.com/confluentinc
Linkedin: https://www.linkedin.com/company/confluent
GitHub: https://github.com/confluentinc
Site: https://developer.confluent.io

ABOUT CONFLUENT DEVELOPER
Confluent Developer provides comprehensive resources for developers looking to learn about Apache Kafka®, Apache Flink®, Confluent Cloud, Confluent Platform, and any other technology related to the broader Data Streaming Platform. Content on Confluent Developer includes courses, getting started guides, topical deep-dives, patterns, tutorials, and listings of community events. Learn more at https://developer.confluent.io. 

#apachekafka #apacheflink #confluent
